science.title: CiQUS researchers develop flexible chemical strategy to create biomimetic synthetic cells
science.description: A team at the Center for Research in Biological Chemistry and Molecular Materials (CiQUS) at the University of Santiago de Compostela has developed a more flexible chemical strategy to create biomimetic structures—artificial systems that mimic cellular functions.

science.title: Reid Wiseman’s Children: Meet the Artemis 2 Astronaut’s Daughters
science.description: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of Artemis II, is a dad of two daughters: Ellie and Katherine. Learn more about his family here.

science.title: New ruthenium-based active agent enables cancer treatment in oxygen-depleted tumors
science.description: Most tumors grow so rapidly that vascular growth cannot keep up, and oxygen-depleted areas form within them. A new active agent could make it possible to treat them with photodynamic therapy.

science.title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
science.description: Lasers could one day steer solar sails and adjust a satellite’s position in outer space, thanks to graphene. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster ride showed how this innovative material has the potential to revolutionise propulsion beyond Earth.

science.title: Apollo astronaut Jim Lovell sends message to Artemis II before flyby
science.description: Astronaut Jim Lovell, who flew on two Apollo-era missions in 1968 and 1970, recorded a message for the Artemis II crew before his death in 2025.

science.title: ‘Payoff day’: Jenni Gibbons on watching Artemis II crew make lunar history
science.description: The sometimes bated breath and fatigue aren't hindering the Calgary-born astronaut from taking in the historic mission from deep inside a NASA control room in Houston.

science.title: Tiny Robots Built From DNA Could Hunt Viruses and Deliver Drugs Inside the Human Body
science.description: A recent review published in the journal SmartBot says these DNA nanorobots could one day be capable of delivering drugs to specific locations in the body, capturing viruses like SARS-CoV-2, and even helping build tiny computing devices.
